'''Aaron Smith''' (born 10 September 1982) is a former professional [[rugby league]] player. He played as a {{rlp|hk}} and {{rlp|lf}}. He played for the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]], [[Leigh Centurions]] and [[Sheffield Eagles]].

==Playing career==
Smith debuted for [[Bradford Bulls]] in May 2003.<ref>{{cite web |title=Smith dazzles on his debut |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8014349.smith-dazzles-on-his-debut/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=3 January 2026 |date=19 May 2003}}</ref>

Having won [[Super League VIII]], Bradford played against 2003 NRL Premiers, the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]].<ref>{{cite news |author=<!-- not stated --> |date=February 13, 2004 |title=Peacock: Smith is our secret weapon |url=https://www.newspapers.com/article/telegraph-and-argus-peacock-smith-is-ou/181141347/ |work=[[Telegraph and Argus]] |access-date=September 15, 2025}}</ref> Smith played at {{rlp|hk}} in the Bulls' 22–4 victory. That year he also captained the Bradford Bulls Academy to victory against the Leeds Rhinos Academy in the Grand final, winning the man of the match and lifting the trophy.

In October 2004, Smith signed a two-year contract with [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Rhinos in no rush to splash cash |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/BL/0005104/20041022/282/0060 |work=Yorkshire Evening Post |date=22 October 2004 |page=60 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref>
